title: FSA Page Tables Extraction
description: Structured output for OCR extraction of FSA form pages (e.g. FSA-2001). Supports both schedule-based (page 7+) and balance-sheet grid (page 6) formats. Each fill-in or data cell with no value must use "cell_type": "value", "answer": "".

section.description: Human-readable section title, e.g. Farm Liabilities Schedules 5A-5D + Personal Liabilities Schedules 6A-6C, or PART H — Balance Sheet (Summary of Schedules).

cells.description: Ordered list of table cells (labels and values).

section_totals.description: Optional page-specific totals keyed by section id.

applicant.description: Optional applicant name (e.g. page 6 balance sheet).

as_of_date.description: Optional as-of date (e.g. page 6 balance sheet).

value_cell_x_inference.description: Optional metadata for inferred value-cell coordinates (page 6 style).

cell.description: A single table cell. Use cell_type "label" for headers/column titles and "value" for data cells. For empty fill-in cells use "cell_type": "value", "answer": "". Coords may include "inferred": true when the bounding box was inferred (e.g. from grid or header position).

schedule.description: Schedule or section id (page 7+ style), e.g. header, 4F, 5A, 5B, 6A.

label.description: Human-readable description of the cell (e.g. column name, row/col description, or "$ Market Value — …" for value cells).

text.description: Alternative to label (page 6 style); raw text content for label cells.

answer.description: Extracted value. Use empty string "" for empty or unfilled cells.

cell_type.description: label = header/column title; value = data/fill-in cell. Empty data cells must be "value" with "answer": "".

row.description: Optional row index (page 6 balance-sheet grid format).

col.description: Optional column index: integer (e.g. 0, 1, 2) or string (e.g. "1_value", "3_value") for value columns in page 6 format.

side.description: Optional side of balance sheet (page 6 format); only for value cells in two-column layout.

inferred.description: True when bounding box was inferred rather than from OCR (e.g. from table grid or header position).
